burial.plot_notes: Trinity Cemetery, Erie PA — with wife Matilda Shadley Unger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Matilda E Shadley Unger (I00673, 1889-1978). Four children: Florence F Unger McGraw (1915-2013 age 97), Elvira M Unger (1916-1918 toddler death in 1918 pandemic), Viola M Unger (1921-2011), Rita M Unger Minnis (1923-2011). Children to be built later stages.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost two-year-old daughter Elvira to 1918 pandemic
narrative_blocks.body: Ransom Unger and his wife Matilda lost their second daughter Elvira at age two in 1918 — the year of the global influenza pandemic that killed more Americans than World War I. Their three other daughters all lived into their eighties or nineties: Florence to ninety-seven, Viola to ninety, Rita to eighty-eight. The pandemic was particularly devastating for healthy young adults and children.

narrative_blocks.header: Left Meadville for Erie, died there at eighty-eight
narrative_blocks.body: Ransom was born in Vernon Township Crawford County in 1888, three years before his father Harmon's death. Sometime after marrying Matilda Shadley he moved north to Erie Pennsylvania, where their later daughters were born and where the couple lived until death. He died October 9, 1976 at Erie at eighty-eight.

burial.plot_notes: Trinity Cemetery, Erie PA — with wife Matilda Shadley Unger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Matilda E Shadley Unger (I00673, 1889-1978). Four children: Florence F Unger McGraw (1915-2013 age 97), Elvira M Unger (1916-1918 toddler death in 1918 pandemic), Viola M Unger (1921-2011), Rita M Unger Minnis (1923-2011). Children to be built later stages.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost two-year-old daughter Elvira to 1918 pandemic
narrative_blocks.body: Ransom Unger and his wife Matilda lost their second daughter Elvira at age two in 1918 — the year of the global influenza pandemic that killed more Americans than World War I. Their three other daughters all lived into their eighties or nineties: Florence to ninety-seven, Viola to ninety, Rita to eighty-eight. The pandemic was particularly devastating for healthy young adults and children.

narrative_blocks.header: Left Meadville for Erie, died there at eighty-eight
narrative_blocks.body: Ransom was born in Vernon Township Crawford County in 1888, three years before his father Harmon's death. Sometime after marrying Matilda Shadley he moved north to Erie Pennsylvania, where their later daughters were born and where the couple lived until death. He died October 9, 1976 at Erie at eighty-eight.

burial.plot_notes: Trinity Cemetery, Erie PA — with husband Ransom Andrew Unger.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost toddler Elvira same year as Armistice
narrative_blocks.body: Matilda Shadley and her husband Ransom lost their two-year-old daughter Elvira in 1918 — the same year that saw the global influenza pandemic kill more Americans than World War I and the November Armistice end the war itself. Their three surviving daughters lived into their late eighties and nineties. Matilda outlived her husband by two years, dying December 26, 1978.

bio.photo_notes: Portrait of Florence F. Unger McGraw (1915-2013).

burial.plot_notes: Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Erie PA — buried with son Donald McGraw (Vietnam Navy vet).

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Dudley J. McGraw (I00681, 1918-1975). Two sons: James McGraw (wife Linda, Erie) + Donald McGraw (HD4293, Vietnam Navy vet). James not built — minimal data.

career.notes: Worked as dietitian at St. Vincent Health Center Erie PA. Member Our Lady of Fatima Roman Catholic Church Canadohta Lake.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost two-year-old sister Elvira in 1918 pandemic
narrative_blocks.body: Florence was three years old in 1918 when her two-year-old sister Elvira died during the global influenza pandemic. She lived another ninety-five years, dying in 2013 at ninety-seven — the eldest and longest-lived of Ransom and Matilda Unger's four daughters. Her son Donald McGraw would serve as a Navy man in Vietnam.

narrative_blocks.header: Dietitian career at Erie's largest hospital
narrative_blocks.body: Florence worked as a dietitian at St. Vincent Health Center in Erie Pennsylvania — the city where she would die in 2013. She was a member of Our Lady of Fatima Roman Catholic Church at Canadohta Lake. She outlived her husband Dudley by thirty-eight years and two of her three sisters.

burial.plot_notes: Calvary Cemetery and Mausoleum, Millcreek Township Erie County PA.

narrative_blocks.header: Died fifty-six, son recently back from Vietnam
narrative_blocks.body: Dudley McGraw died in 1975 at fifty-six or fifty-seven — when his only documented son Donald was twenty-nine and recently returned from Vietnam War Navy service. His wife Florence outlived him by thirty-eight years until her 2013 death at ninety-seven. He is buried at Calvary Cemetery in Millcreek Township Erie County, while his widow and Navy-vet son would be buried at the nearby Gate of Heaven Cemetery.

burial.plot_notes: Gate of Heaven Cemetery, Erie PA — with mother Florence.

career.notes: Thirty-six years at Eriez Magnetics in Erie PA, retiring in 2008. Enjoyed fishing.

military_service.notes: U.S. Navy during the Vietnam War per his 2015 obituary. Specific rank, ship, and tour dates undocumented.

narrative_blocks.header: Vietnam Navy veteran, thirty-six years at Eriez Magnetics
narrative_blocks.body: Donald McGraw served in the U.S. Navy during the Vietnam War as a young man. He returned home to work at Eriez Magnetics in Erie Pennsylvania for thirty-six years, retiring in 2008. He died seven years later in Erie at sixty-nine, survived by his wife Judith of thirty-six years and three adult children.

narrative_blocks.header: Postwar Erie birth, father dead at fifty-six
narrative_blocks.body: Donald was born February 15, 1946 in Erie Pennsylvania — during the surge of postwar births that followed World War II. His father Dudley died in 1975 when Donald was twenty-nine and only recently returned from Vietnam. He outlived his ninety-seven-year-old mother Florence by only two years.

narrative_blocks.header: Died age two in 1918 influenza pandemic
narrative_blocks.body: Elvira M. Unger died in 1918 at age two during the global influenza pandemic that killed more Americans than World War I. Her three sisters Florence, Viola, and Rita all lived into their eighties or nineties — Florence reaching ninety-seven.

narrative_blocks.header: Lived ninety years, sister-pair die same year
narrative_blocks.body: Viola Unger lived from 1921 to 2011 — into her ninetieth year. She and her younger sister Rita Minnis died the same year in 2011, leaving their elder sister Florence McGraw alone among the four original Unger daughters for two more years until Florence's 2013 death at ninety-seven. No documented marriage.

burial.plot_notes: Saint Michaels Cemetery, Greenville Mercer County PA — with husband Donald M. Minnis.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Donald M. Minnis (I00682, 1920-2009 veteran). Per his obit: 6 children built later stages — Daniel + Beverly Bressler + Christine Kotapish + Barbara Jean Fisher + Darwin A. Minnis + Michael A. Minnis. 19 grandchildren, 14 great-grandchildren, 4 great-great-grandchildren at his 2009 death.

narrative_blocks.header: Born Lancaster Pennsylvania, far from family base
narrative_blocks.body: Rita was born April 27, 1923 in Lancaster County Pennsylvania — surprising given her family's deep roots in Crawford and Venango counties on the western side of the state. Her parents Ransom and Matilda may have been traveling or temporarily living in Lancaster around her birth. She would eventually return there after her 1946 marriage to Donald Minnis.

narrative_blocks.header: Married Armistice Day 1946, six children spread cross-country
narrative_blocks.body: Rita married Donald M. Minnis on November 11, 1946 — Armistice Day, which would be renamed Veterans Day eight years later in 1954. They had six children: three sons and three daughters spread between Pennsylvania, Ohio, Montana, and Illinois.

burial.plot_notes: Saint Michaels Cemetery, Greenville Mercer County PA — with wife Rita Unger Minnis.

career.notes: Five years at Tonka Toys Manufacturing Lancaster PA.
Twenty years at Millersville State University.
Ten years in Lancaster Ohio before retirement.

military_service.notes: Designated 'Veteran' on FindAGrave but specific war, branch, rank, and dates of service undocumented. Born 1920 → most likely WWII-era veteran given age (21 at Pearl Harbor) but not confirmed in available sources. Member Jamestown VFW Post 5424.

narrative_blocks.header: Veteran with three distinct careers in retirement
narrative_blocks.body: Donald Minnis served as a veteran — war undocumented but likely WWII given his 1920 Meadville birth. He had three distinct working careers in succession: ten years in Lancaster Ohio, twenty years at Millersville State University, and five years at Tonka Toys Manufacturing of Lancaster Pennsylvania. He was active at St. Margaret Roman Catholic Church Jamestown and the Jamestown VFW Post 5424.

narrative_blocks.header: Sixty-two years married, four generations alive at death
narrative_blocks.body: Donald married Rita Unger on November 11, 1946 — Armistice Day. They had six children, nineteen grandchildren, fourteen great-grandchildren, and four great-great-grandchildren by his 2009 death — a rare survival into the fifth living generation. He also tutored young people at Family Services and helped at the Jamestown Food Pantry.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — with father-in-law John Ellis and wife Florence Hannah Ellis Unger McFarland.

narrative_blocks.header: Minnesota-born settler died Pennsylvania at thirty-six
narrative_blocks.body: Harmon Demmington Unger was born March 24, 1857 in Minnesota — far from his eventual Pennsylvania home — and migrated east to Crawford County, where he married Florence Hannah Ellis in 1881. They had four children between 1882 and 1889. He died in Pennsylvania on January 29, 1894 at thirty-six, leaving Florence widowed with four children aged five to twelve.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA — with father John Ellis and first husband Harmon Unger.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Harmon Demmington Unger (I00668, 1857 MN - 1894 PA) — four children born to this marriage: Rush John Unger (1882), Bertha Mae Unger Young (1884), Ransom Andrew Unger (1888), Irvin Clarence Unger (1889). Children to be built Stage 6Y-91.

naming_inspiration.description: Middle name Hannah honors her paternal grandmother Hannah Ellis Ellis (1812-1876), who was alive at her birth and likely her godmother.

narrative_blocks.header: Born in Pennsylvania's most famous oil ghost town
narrative_blocks.body: Florence Hannah Ellis was born July 12, 1864 at Pithole City Venango County Pennsylvania — six months before oil was struck at the nearby Frazier Well in January 1865. The strike turned her birthplace into a boomtown of fifteen thousand with fifty hotels and the third-busiest post office in Pennsylvania, only to collapse by 1870 when the wells ran dry. By the time she could remember it, Pithole was already a ghost town.

narrative_blocks.header: Lost mother at seven, twice widowed by fifty
narrative_blocks.body: Florence's mother Lois Olive Hibbard died at twenty-four in 1871, five days before Florence's seventh birthday. Her father remarried and the second wife Elsie Ann Jolly also died young, leaving Florence's year-old half-brother John Clarence motherless in 1885. Florence herself would be twice widowed by age fifty — first husband Harmon Unger died at thirty-six in 1894, second husband Reuben Bishop McFarland outlived her by five years until 1938.

narrative_blocks.header: Married at seventeen, four children by thirty
narrative_blocks.body: Florence married Harmon Demmington Unger in 1881 at age seventeen — a Minnesota-born settler who had migrated to Crawford County Pennsylvania. They had four children between 1882 and 1889: Rush John, Bertha Mae, Ransom Andrew, and Irvin Clarence. When Harmon died in 1894 at thirty-six, she was thirty with four children aged five to twelve.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford Co PA — same cemetery as his maternal great-uncle Dr. Edward Ellis MD's two wives Mary Kennedy and Sarah Buchanan.

marriages.spouse_research_notes: Lois Olive Hibbard Ellis (1846-1871, died age 24) — to be built as I-prefix in Stage 6Y-90 along with daughter Florence Hannah Ellis McFarland (1864-1933).
Elsie Ann Jolly Ellis (1859-1885, died age 25-26) — to be built as I-prefix in Stage 6Y-90 along with son John Clarence Ellis (1884-1977).
Mrs. Florence Ellis (unknown-1930) — m. 1900, possibly already a widow with the surname Ellis at the time of marriage. To be built as I-prefix in Stage 6Y-90.

narrative_blocks.header: Daughter born Pithole forest before oil strike
narrative_blocks.body: John Ellis and his young wife Lois were living in the forested farmland of Venango County Pennsylvania when their only daughter Florence Hannah was born July 12, 1864 — six months before oil was struck at the nearby Frazier Well in January 1865. Within months the discovery exploded the area into Pithole City, a boomtown of fifteen thousand with fifty hotels, three churches, a daily newspaper, and the third-busiest post office in Pennsylvania. By 1870 the wells had run dry and Pithole was essentially a ghost town.

narrative_blocks.header: Followed Ellis family network west to Meadville
narrative_blocks.body: After leaving the collapsing Pithole oil settlement John Ellis moved his family to Meadville Pennsylvania, where his maternal great-uncle Dr. Edward Ellis MD had practiced medicine for forty years already. He lived in Meadville the rest of his life — over four decades — and was buried at Greendale Cemetery alongside the graves of Edward's two deceased wives, who had died in 1840 and 1844.

narrative_blocks.header: Survived three wives, two died young
narrative_blocks.body: John Ellis was widowed three times: first wife Lois Olive Hibbard died at twenty-four in 1871 leaving him their daughter Florence Hannah aged seven, and second wife Elsie Ann Jolly died at twenty-six in 1885 leaving him their year-old son John Clarence. He married a third time in 1900 at age sixty to a widow surnamed Ellis.

narrative_blocks.header: Only surviving child of first-cousin parents
narrative_blocks.body: John was the only one of his parents Hannah and Noah Ellis's four children to marry and have descendants. His older sister Sarah died unmarried at twenty-four in 1871, and his infant twin sisters Mary and Martha had both died in 1851.

burial.plot_notes: Greendale Cemetery, Meadville Crawford County PA.

narrative_blocks.header: Died at twenty-four, daughter not yet seven
narrative_blocks.body: Lois Olive Hibbard died on July 7, 1871 at age twenty-four — five days before her only daughter Florence Hannah would have turned seven. The same year her sister-in-law Sarah Ellis also died unmarried at twenty-four in Massachusetts, suggesting possible epidemic conditions. Lois left her husband John Ellis a widower with their young daughter, who would grow up in Meadville visiting her mother's grave at Greendale Cemetery.

burialCemetery.name: Trinity Cemetery (Erie)
burialCemetery.notes: Trinity Cemetery, Erie PA. Burial site of Ransom Andrew Unger (HD4277, gen 9 Hooker descendant) and his wife Matilda Shadley Unger.
